name: User Details
summary: Retrieves the logged-in user's account details: enabled exchanges (exarr), enabled price/order types (orarr), enabled products as an array of Product Obj (prarr), broker id, branch id, email, account id, mobile number, user privilege (always INVESTOR), and access type. POST call with jData (JSON containing uid) and jKey (session token).

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

response_fields.name: stat
response_fields.description: User details success or failure indication.

response_fields.name: exarr
response_fields.description: Json array of strings with enabled exchange names

response_fields.name: orarr
response_fields.description: Json array of strings with enabled price types for user

response_fields.name: prarr
response_fields.description: Json array of Product Obj with enabled products, as defined below.

response_fields.name: uprev
response_fields.description: Always it will be an INVESTOR, other types of user not allowed to login using this API.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: It will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors.

response_fields.name: prd
response_fields.description: Product Obj field (PRODUCT OBJ FORMAT): Product name

response_fields.name: s_prdt_ali
response_fields.description: Product Obj field (PRODUCT OBJ FORMAT): Product display name

response_fields.name: exch
response_fields.description: Product Obj field (PRODUCT OBJ FORMAT): Json array of strings with enabled, allowed exchange names

notes:
- The doc shows the curl example with the placeholder host 'https://BaseURL/UserDetails'; the actual base URL per the prose is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI/UserDetails.
- jData is sent as a URL-style form parameter: jData={...json...}&jKey=... in the request body (a Content-Type: application/json header is shown even though the body is form-encoded jData=/jKey=).
- prarr is an array of Product Obj entries; each Product Obj has fields prd / s_prdt_ali / exch, documented separately under PRODUCT OBJ FORMAT (page 85): prd = Product name, s_prdt_ali = Product display name, exch = Json array of enabled/allowed exchange names. Sample products: C=Delivery, I=Intraday, H=High Leverage, B=Bracket Order.
- orarr sample enumerates price/order types: LMT, SL-LMT, DS, 2L, 3L, 4L.
- uprev is always 'INVESTOR'; other user types cannot log in via this API. (The doc table leaves uprev's Possible-value cell blank; INVESTOR appears only in its description and in the sample.)
- The doc table leaves the Description cell BLANK for the email and actid response fields (reproduced as empty); their meaning is inferred only from the sample.
- request_time appears only on success; emsg appears only on errors. access_type and m_num are documented but do not appear in the sample success response.
- The success-response JSON in the source has a stray leading comma before the third prarr entry (', { "prd":"H"') — reproduced verbatim; it is a doc artifact, not valid JSON. The source also renders the prarr block with curly/smart quotes; straight quotes are used here for consistency.
issues: The only request json field is uid (required). Verified against page images (pp. 83-85). Corrections vs draft: (1) removed the invented possible_values 'INVESTOR' on uprev — the doc's Possible-value cell is blank, INVESTOR is in the description only; (2) blanked the email and actid response descriptions to match the source table (the doc leaves those Description cells empty; draft had inferred 'Email.'/'Account Id.'); (3) corrected stat possible_values to the verbatim 'Ok or Not_Ok'; (4) removed trailing periods the draft added to jData/uid descriptions (source has none); (5) corrected the Product Obj field descriptions (prd/s_prdt_ali/exch) to the verbatim page-85 wording and removed the draft's parenthetical examples. The success sample preserves the doc's doubled-comma typo before the 'H' product entry verbatim.
